1.11a: Mortgage Centre details are on the letter of instruction.
Standard documents are available at www.natwest.com/conveyancers.
Use LMS portal for contact regarding pending completions.
Email is the preferred contact method for all other non-portal queries.
For Offset Flexible Mortgages, contact the firm listed on the instruction letter.
1.11b: Contact methods are the same as specified in 1.11a.
1.7: Lending is not permitted when the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: The firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the seller, unless a separate fee earner or partner within the firm acts for the lender.
1.15: The firm may not act if the person handling the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower, unless a separate fee earner or partner within the firm acts for the lender.
3.1.3: Do not provide the seller's solicitor details unless specifically requested.
3.1.4: Notify the lender via the contact details in 1.11a if the seller lacks legal representation for a decision on whether to proceed.
3.1.5: Acceptable ID documents include: old-style UK driving licence, Inland Revenue notification, and Construction Industry tax exemption certificate.
3.2.3: Do not provide the seller's solicitor details unless specifically requested.
3.2.4: Notify the lender via the contact details in 1.11a if the seller lacks legal representation.
4.5a: Re-inspection requests go to the Mortgage Centre (1.11a).
Offset Flexible Mortgage: RBS Offset Completions Team, Ground Floor, Lakeside 300, Old Chapel Way, Broadland Business Park, Norwich NR7 0WG. Telephone: 0345 300 4013. Fax: 0845 366 0498.
4.5b: Submit Certificate of Title via LMS portal if instructed through LMS.
Non-LMS cases: send to Mortgage Centre (1.11a).
Offset Flexible Mortgage: RBS Offset Completions Team, Norwich (address in 4.5a).
4.1: A valuation report is provided only if a physical internal inspection was performed.
If no physical inspection occurred, the lender will not share the report; queries should be directed to the customer.
4.3: Contact point for incorrect valuer assumptions is the same as 1.11a.
5.1.1: If the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months, report: seller's name/address, purchase price, connections between parties, and details of any work carried out since purchase.
5.2.1: Contact point for sellers who are not the registered owner is 1.11a.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
Report proximity to HS2 only if less than 300 metres.
Report mining entries only if less than 20 metres and insurance cannot be obtained on a normal basis.
5.4.5: Personal searches are acceptable if the agent has professional indemnity insurance and a clear Certificate of Title can be provided.
5.4.6: Search insurance is acceptable if it protects the lender and title is good and marketable.
5.5.3a: Contact point for reporting breaches or unsatisfied conditions is 1.11a.
5.5.3b: Original or copies of planning permission are not required.
5.5.3c: Original or copies of building regulation consents are not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required.
5.5.4: Contact point for reporting restrictions affecting value or marketability is 1.11a.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable if positive covenants for maintenance/insurance are enforceable. Max LTV is 90%.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are acceptable if positive covenants for maintenance/insurance are enforceable. Max LTV is 90%.
5.7.1c: Report freehold flats or flying freeholds to contact 1.11a.
5.8.1: Acceptable if building has max 4 flats, borrower occupies one, and owns/shares the freehold.
5.8.5: Security must include both the leasehold flat and the freehold reversion to the other flat.
5.9.1: The lender does not lend on commonhold property.
5.10.1: Contact point for restrictions on use is 1.11a.
5.13.1: Parental/Guardian gifts or deposits are acceptable; second charges in their favour are permitted.
Foreign nationals without permanent right to reside must use their own resources.
Non-parental third-party gifts are acceptable only if non-repayable and no second charge is allowed.
Seller gifts/incentives may affect the purchase price or maximum loan amount.
5.14.15a: Contact point for absentee/insolvent landlords is 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absent or insolvent landlords.
5.14.17a: Building safety documentation is not required unless specified in 5.14.17b.
5.14.17b: Notify the lender if remediation works under the Building Safety Act 2022 are required.
Notify the lender if the owner is a non-qualifying leaseholder or if remediation costs are anticipated.
Requirements can be disregarded if building is under 11m, has fewer than 5 storeys, or has no cladding/fire safety issues.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term is the mortgage term plus 30 years.
5.14.9: Ground rents treated as ASTs under the Housing Act 1988 do not require reporting if the lease is varied or indemnity insurance is in place.
Onerous ground rents (above RPI, doubling in less than 25 years) must be reported.
New qualifying long leases must have ground rent no more than a peppercorn per year.
Lender does not review indemnity policy terms but requires a clean COT.
5.14.10: Contact point for service charge matters is 1.11a.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms, but varying the lease is preferred.
5.14.12: Ground rent/service charge receipts are not required for standard mortgages.
Offset Flexible Mortgages require receipts after completion.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service is not required.
5.15.2a: Contact point for management company problems is 1.11a.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates are not required.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association are not required.
5.16.2: Contact point for search entry issues is 1.11a.
5.17.5a: Certified copy of Power of Attorney must be sent after completion.
5.17.5b: Certified copy of statutory declaration of non-revocation must be sent after completion.
5.19.1: Lending on shared ownership is only permitted if the borrower will own 100% at completion.
Affordable housing or shared equity proposals must be reported to 1.11a.
5.20.1: Report if solar PV panel roof space leases do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.4: Details of existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed to 1.11a.
6.4.4: Builder incentives for new builds are the only accepted incentives.
Report incentives totaling more than 5% of the purchase price.
Do not send the UK Finance Disclosure Form.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements for standard ASTs are not required.
Non-AST agreements must be sent and must include a break clause, no security of tenure, mortgage recognition, and a max 3-year term.
6.6.4: The lender does not lend on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO).
6.7.1: Acceptable 10-year warranties: NHBC, Zurich, BLP, Build Zone, BuildCare, Ward Cole (12yr), LABC, Premier Guarantee (refer flats), Global Home, The Q Policy, BOPAS, Protek, Advantage, ICW, One Guarantee, CADIS, CIR, Self-Build Zone.
Protek Self Build Warranty is accepted but Customer Build Warranty is excluded.
6.7.4: Lender will proceed without a warranty if a Professional Consultant's Certificate in the UK Finance form is issued prior to exchange.
6.8.1: Report unadopted roads with: adoption proposals, condition, maintenance costs, access rights, and confirmation borrower understands liabilities.
6.12.1: Lending is not permitted on properties subject to overage agreements.
7.3: Consents required for all occupants 17+ except borrower's children under 26.
Lifetime Mortgages require spouses/civil partners to be joint owners/mortgagors.
8.1: Conveyancers may not advise third-party guarantors or occupiers; independent legal advice must be recommended.
9.1: Do not send indemnity policies; provide full details on the Certificate of Title.
9.2: Indemnity insurance limit must equal the property value.
10.2b: Minimum of 7 working days notice is required for mortgage advance.
10.3: CHAPS Fee is a standard deduction.
10.7: Delayed completion: Return funds via CHAPS to Sort Code 83-05-00, Account 98090165 by the next working day.
10.11: Interest is charged at the mortgage rate from the date of fund release until recredited to the lender.
14.1.5: Do not send original mortgage deeds to the lender.
If registration is not complete within 30 days, update LMS every 30 days with the reason for delay.
14.2.2: LMS instructed cases must upload: Priority Notice, Confirmation of Application to Register, Official Copy, and (if applicable) Power of Attorney, Guarantee, and Occupier Consent.
Non-LMS cases: send Deed of Consent, PoA, and Statutory Declaration only.
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: 'Signed and delivered as a Deed for and on behalf of the Bank by a duly authorised signatory.'
16.3.1: Reconstitution of security is required in Transfer of Equity cases.
17.1.1: Redemption statements for RBS Personal Mortgages must be requested via LMS Secure Link.
Offset Mortgages: submit written request to Norwich address or email CMSRedemptionsTeam@natwest.com.
17.2.1a: Repayment remittance: Mortgage Centre (PO Box 123, Greenock) or Offset Team (Norwich).
Lender arranges discharge for registered land; no written confirmation of release is provided.
